name: IAEA NSS-17-G
fullName: Technical Guidance for Computer Security at Nuclear Facilities
description: Global IAEA recommendations establishing computer security plans for nuclear material holding systems.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Nuclear Facility CDA Vetting
questions.text: Are all computer-based systems important to safety identified and cataloged?
questions.description:
Verify systematic identification of nuclear safety-critical computer-based systems. Review inventory databases.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the cda governance configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Computer Security Strategy Approval
questions.text: Is the facility computer security strategy formally approved by executive management?
questions.description:
Verify documented program approvals, management signatures, and evidence of periodic review cycles.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the cda governance configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Process Zone Segregation Strategy
questions.text: Are safety-critical systems isolated inside defined security zones (enforced by Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches, network zoning firewalls, and isolated Purdue model level boundaries)?
questions.description:
Verify network topology drawings, logical boundaries, and stateful router configurations.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the cda perimeter segmentation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Inadequate network segmentation allows IT-OT convergence traffic to flow unmediated across enclaves. A malware infection on the corporate LAN (like ransomware) can propagate directly to critical process control loops, halting operations.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Logical Gateway Separation
questions.text: Are all external logical connections routed through a stateful firewall conduit (enforced by Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches, network zoning firewalls, and isolated Purdue model level boundaries)?
questions.description:
Inspect boundary firewall routing rules, verify VLAN separation, and audit gateway controls.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the cda perimeter segmentation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Inadequate network segmentation allows IT-OT convergence traffic to flow unmediated across enclaves. A malware infection on the corporate LAN (like ransomware) can propagate directly to critical process control loops, halting operations.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Control Center Access Control Locks
questions.text: Are physical access perimeters and entry locks active at safety-critical sites (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Verify physical lock gates, check badge-reader entry logs, and review camera feeds.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the physical access control configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical IAEA NSS 17 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Physical Access Vetting Logging
questions.text: Are physical entry visitor logs archived for at least 90 days (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Check card reader access databases, check reader logs, and verify audit records.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the physical access control configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical IAEA NSS 17 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Safety System Logic Backup Logs
questions.text: Are backups of safety-critical system logic executed weekly (aligned with incident response playbooks, offsite backups, and isolated write-once media)?
questions.description:
Review backup logs, verify automated update schedules, and check backup success registries.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the operations recovery configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Failing to maintain isolated, offline backups during convergence events risks catastrophic downtime during ransomware outbreaks. If backups reside on the shared enterprise domain, the same malware that encrypts SCADA HMIs will wipe the recovery configurations.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Logic Restoration Testing Strategy
questions.text: Are backup restoration procedures tested annually for safety PLCs (aligned with incident response playbooks, offsite backups, and isolated write-once media)?
questions.description:
Validate backup recovery test logs, offsite replica configurations, and restoration reports.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the operations recovery configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Failing to maintain isolated, offline backups during convergence events risks catastrophic downtime during ransomware outbreaks. If backups reside on the shared enterprise domain, the same malware that encrypts SCADA HMIs will wipe the recovery configurations.

questions.name: IAEA NSS-17-G - Vendor Remote Link Tunnels Control

Validate that remote vendor connections route through Jump Hosts with visual logging.
